summary: Frontend-only test of banquet cart hold countdown + warning UX. Verified BanquetCheckout sticky-header countdown pill, warning banner, and extend button. Found two HIGH-priority gaps: (1) the FAB countdown pill is never visible on /services/banquet because BanquetResults passes hideFab=true to EventCartDrawer — the countdown lives inside the hidden FAB only, and the sticky banquet-cart-strip doesn't surface it; (2) clicking the extend-hold button on the checkout page does NOT reset the timer back to 10:00 — countdown kept ticking (0:26 -> 0:24 after click) and the amber banner stayed up. Could not validate the drawer-side hero pill / warning banner because the test could not open the drawer via the (hidden) FAB; the sticky strip needs its own opener testid.

ui_bugs.issue: FAB is rendered with hideFab=true, so [data-testid=event-cart-countdown-fab] is NEVER mounted on /services/banquet. The countdown M:SS pill is only inside the floating Cart Button. The visible sticky strip (banquet-cart-strip) has no countdown pill at all -> the visual cart-hold timer is invisible until the drawer is opened.

Clicking [data-testid=co-extend-hold] does NOT reset the timer to 10:00. Observed: countdown text went '0:26' (pre-click) -> '0:24' (1.8s post-click, force-clicked). co-expiry-warning banner remained visible. extendHold in useEventCart.js stamps last_active_at = Date.now(), and the countdown useEffect depends on cart.last_active_at, so the click is either not firing or the state update isn't propagating. Same code path is used by event-cart-extend-hold in the drawer — likely broken there too.

integration_issues.issue: Drawer FAB is hidden (hideFab=true) and there is no documented testid on the sticky cart strip's open-drawer button, so the drawer cannot be opened programmatically by automation. Could not exercise event-cart-countdown-hero / event-cart-expiry-warning / event-cart-extend-hold from /services/banquet via the deployed UI.

issues: No visible cart-hold countdown anywhere in the persistent UI until the drawer is opened — defeats the purpose of surfacing the 10-min hold.

verified_working: [data-testid=co-cart-countdown] renders with 'Hold expires in M:SS' text
co-cart-countdown switches to amber + animate-pulse classes when expiresInSeconds <= 120
[data-testid=co-expiry-warning] amber banner renders with 'Your event hold expires in X:XX' copy when <=2 min remaining
[data-testid=co-extend-hold] button renders inside the warning banner
Banquet checkout page loads and shows the step indicator with sticky header (no console errors related to cart-hold UX)
action_items: BanquetResults: either flip hideFab to false on /services/banquet, OR add a duplicate countdown pill (data-testid=event-cart-countdown-fab or equivalent) directly into the banquet-cart-strip so the timer is always visible.

Debug why clicking co-extend-hold / event-cart-extend-hold does not reset last_active_at. Likely candidates: (a) the click is being intercepted/no-op, (b) onClick prop isn't actually wired through, (c) extendHold closure stale. Add a quick console.log inside extendHold and verify in DevTools. Manual repro: seed cart with last_active_at = Date.now() - 9.5*60*1000, open /services/banquet/checkout, click 'Extend hold +10 min' — the pill should jump to 10:00 within ~1s but it doesn't.
Once extendHold reset is fixed, retest event-cart-extend-hold inside the drawer too (it uses the same hook action).
Add a data-testid to the sticky banquet-cart-strip's open-drawer button so automation can reach the drawer without relying on the FAB.
critical_code_review_comments: EventCartDrawer renders the countdown pill ONLY inside the FAB Button; when hideFab=true (BanquetResults case) the pill becomes inaccessible. Surface should be moved to a component that's always visible regardless of hideFab, or duplicated into the parent sticky strip.

useEventCart.extendHold uses setCart(c => ({...c, last_active_at: Date.now()})). That should work, but the observed UI shows the timer doesn't reset — suggests the handler isn't reaching that line. Worth adding a unit-style click trace.
Many 429 'Too Many Requests' responses observed in console on banquet route — orthogonal to this task but worth a separate look (rate limiter likely hammered by repeated permissions fetches).

success_rate.frontend: ~40% (checkout pill+warning render; FAB pill missing on Results; extend-hold does not reset timer)

seed_data_creation: Used localStorage seeding of oryno:event-cart to simulate warning state (last_active_at = now - 9m30s) and fresh state (last_active_at = now).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login is two-step: Login to Your Account -> email -> Continue -> password -> Sign In. After login, navigate to /services/banquet or /services/banquet/checkout and seed localStorage via the snippet in the request. The FAB on /services/banquet is hidden — to open the drawer you'll need to click the sticky banquet-cart-strip (currently no testid on its open button). Checkout testids work; their values do not auto-reset on extend click.
rca of the issue: BUG #1 root cause: BanquetResults.jsx passes hideFab=true to EventCartDrawer, which short-circuits the entire SheetTrigger Button JSX where event-cart-countdown-fab lives. The countdown is never rendered to the DOM on that route. Mitigation: render a parallel countdown pill in the sticky banquet-cart-strip (line ~603 of BanquetResults.jsx), or conditionally render the pill outside the FAB Button. BUG #2 root cause: not yet pinpointed in code — extendHold's setCart pattern looks correct, so the most likely culprit is that the onClick isn't actually firing or the button isn't receiving the click in the observed environment. Repro: 30s-remaining seed -> checkout -> click Extend hold +10 min -> expect 10:00, actual 0:24.
